channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Pakistan and Kuwait Negotiate Expanded Defence Cooperation
item.description: Pakistan and Kuwait are in early-stage negotiations for an expanded defence pact, reportedly tied to energy cooperation and investment. The talks, according to Reuters, involve Kuwait seeking a show o...

item.title: Federal Govt Launches Textile Sector Revival Plan to Boost Jobs and Exports in Kaduna
item.description: The Nigerian federal government has unveiled a comprehensive plan to revitalize the struggling textile industry, focusing on Kaduna's Kakuri textile hub. The initiative, part of the National Cotton Te...

item.title: Irom Sharmila urges collective support for Sonam Wangchuck's hunger strike against exam reforms
item.description: Irom Sharmila, a Manipur activist who fasted for 16 years to repeal the Armed Forces (Special Powers) Act, is urging widespread support for Sonam Wangchuck's hunger strike against exam reforms and the...

item.title: Royal Commission's Role in Addressing Antisemitism and Social Cohesion
item.description: The article discusses the Royal Commission into Antisemitism and Social Cohesion, highlighting concerns about its own role in exacerbating antisemitism and social divisions. Commissioner Virginia Bell...

item.title: Witness K charged in R15m precious stones theft investigation
item.description: A former Ekurhuleni Metropolitan Police Department (EMPD) official, identified as Witness K, has been arrested in connection with an alleged R15m precious stones theft that occurred during a 2023 raid...

item.title: Republican Expert Criticizes Trump's Election Speech as Lackluster and Unoriginal
item.description: The article discusses a Republican elections expert, Georgia's Gabriel Sterling, who criticized President Trump's recent election speech for lacking new information. Despite the White House's promise ...

item.title: Ombudsman nears completion of investigation into Romualdez over flood control controversy
item.description: The Office of the Ombudsman has stated it is nearly finished with its fact-finding investigation against former House Speaker Martin Romualdez regarding the flood control controversy. Ombudsman Jesus ...

item.title: Government hikes petrol and diesel prices amid global tensions and rising costs
item.description: The Pakistani government has raised petrol and diesel prices by Rs5.44 and Rs31.05 per litre, respectively, effective immediately for the next three days. This decision follows renewed regional tensio...

item.title: Anglican Church appoints new bishops for key dioceses
item.description: The Church of Nigeria (Anglican Communion) has officially appointed four new bishops to lead dioceses in Benue, Akwa Ibom, Ekiti, and Lagos states. The elections took place during the Episcopal Synod ...

item.title: Xi Jinping Calls for International Cooperation in AI Development at Shanghai Conference
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping has emphasised that artificial intelligence should not be controlled by any single nation, speaking at the World Artificial Intelligence Conference in Shanghai. He describ...

item.title: Canada and Mexico Collaborate to Address U.S. Auto Tariffs
item.description: Canada and Mexico have agreed to collaborate with the U.S. to address auto tariffs, following the expiration of the Canada-United States-Mexico Agreement (CUSMA) by July 1. Mexico's Foreign Affairs Se...

item.title: Mercedes' Antonelli Leads Practice Session Ahead of Belgian Grand Prix
item.description: Kimi Antonelli, the 19-year-old Mercedes driver, set the fastest time in the second practice session for the Belgian Grand Prix, outpacing McLaren's Lando Norris by 0.190 seconds. Norris, who faces a ...

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: England's journey through challenges and opportunities
item.description: The FIFA World Cup 2026 is set to be a pivotal event for England's national football team, as they navigate a complex landscape of domestic and international challenges. The article highlights the tea...

item.title: government hikes petrol and diesel prices amid rising global costs and regional tensions
item.description: The government has increased petrol prices by Rs5.44 and diesel by Rs31.05 per litre effective immediately for the next three days, citing higher import premiums and global prices due to renewed US-Ir...

item.title: Right-wing leaders Farage and Hanson collaborate at UK conservative conference
item.description: Nigel Farage, leader of Reform UK, has publicly endorsed Pauline Hanson following their attendance at a major Conservative Political Action Conference in London. The meeting between the Australian rig...

item.title: Penticton Peach Classic Triathlon Sees Record Participation, Boosting Local Economy
item.description: The 43rd annual Peach Classic Triathlon in Penticton, B.C., is set to attract over 1,100 athletes, a 60% increase from last year, according to organizers. This surge in participation is expected to ge...

item.title: Political Leaders Show Unity at Funeral Service Ahead of 2027 Election
item.description: The funeral service of Mrs Sabainah Ibironke Opawole, mother of Yeye Olufunke Daniel and mother-in-law of former Ogun State Governor Otunba Gbenga Daniel, turned into a political gathering. Key figure...

item.title: CDC Staff Cuts Hinder Cyclospora Outbreak Response
item.description: The article discusses how the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) has faced significant staffing reductions, particularly in its cyclospora outbreak response lab, which was downsized from...

item.title: Winnipeg Blue Bombers to Start Dru Brown Against Ottawa RedBlacks After QB Collaros Injury
item.description: The Winnipeg Blue Bombers have confirmed that quarterback Zach Collaros will miss the team's Week Seven matchup against the Ottawa RedBlacks due to a neck injury, marking his second consecutive game a...

item.title: Protecting Health During Wildfire Smoke Events: A Comprehensive Guide
item.description: Wildfire smoke poses significant health risks due to its particulate matter, which can infiltrate the bloodstream and cause damage to the heart, lungs, and other organs. The article emphasizes the imp...
